behaviors.description: Missing required key from both config file and environment reports exact dotted path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because the failure boundary is the handoff across file load + `TOOL_` env overlay + required-key validator/error formatter, not a single pure function. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because this can be reproduced in-process with a temp config file and patched env, with no subprocess or real network/OS-only primitive required. Integration-brake: (a) no required subprocess/network; local file+env is enough, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires missing-key reporting across file/env sources, not CLI subprocess behavior or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp config file missing `database.host`, clear corresponding `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST`, run the real loader entrypoint with patched env, assert it fails and the emitted message contains exactly `database.host`.

behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because the behavior is about the loader honoring process env and then locating/reading config at the resolved path (module interaction), not only path-string math. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because temp directories plus env patching reproduce the failure without subprocess/network. Integration-brake: (a) no required subprocess/network/OS event surface, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is limited to path resolution under `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, which is verifiable fully in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp dir containing a config with a sentinel value, also place a different config under a fake `~/.config`; run loader and assert it reads the sentinel from `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` location.

behaviors.description: `TOOL_` environment variable overrides conflicting YAML key value.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because real failure appears at merge precedence between parsed YAML config and env-derived overrides across modules. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because this is deterministic in-process wiring with temp file + env patch, no subprocess or external infra needed. Integration-brake: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess boundary beyond normal local file read, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agentic flow, (d) no LLM handoff stages. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is precedence logic between local inputs, not real-HTTP/subprocess/OS-event behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Write YAML with `database.host: file-host`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`, execute loader, assert resulting config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax errors include detected line number in the error message.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because the failure boundary is parser exception propagation/translation into user-facing loader error, involving file read + YAML parser + formatter. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because a malformed temp YAML file triggers the real parser path in-process; no subprocess/network required. Integration-brake: (a) no required external infra or subprocess, (b) no AI/ML dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text asks for parser-detected line reporting, which is captured without full CLI subprocess or external systems.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp config file with invalid YAML at a known line, run loader, assert failure message includes that line number from parser output.

behaviors.description: On first run with no config directory, loader creates directory and writes defaults file before continuing.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this is cross-module side-effect orchestration (path resolution + mkdir + defaults serialization + subsequent load), not single-function logic. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because real failure is catchable with in-process temp filesystem operations; no subprocess, real HTTP, or external API boundary is required by the behavior text. Integration-brake: (a) requires local filesystem writes but not higher-level system harness beyond integration, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is limited to local first-run bootstrap (`creates it and writes a defaults file`), which is fully testable in-process with temp paths.
behaviors.test_description: Start with non-existent temp config dir (via patched home/`XDG_CONFIG_HOME`), run loader, assert directory now exists, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds successfully.
